scope.selection_note: Codex-only post-ingest identity cleanup prep for ROUTE and CORRIDOR duplicate_global_identity rows only. Inputs were current data/canonical/routes.jsonl, data/canonical/corridors.jsonl, data/identity-overrides/merges.json, data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json, and allowed in-process validateRepo duplicate_global_identity output.
scope.write_limit: Only this review note was written. No identity overrides, LOG.md, manifest, canonical JSONL, DB, wiki, submissions, or other files were edited.

proposal_guards: No proposed alias_id may already be present as a merge alias in current data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
No proposed alias_id may be a current merge target in current data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
No proposed target_id may be a current merge alias in current data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
No proposed pair may appear in current data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json.
No route variant proposal was included without source evidence proving lifecycle/same service.
No partial-overlap corridor proposal was included without source-backed same-corridor/project identity.

notable_exclusions.reason: Excluded as a partial-overlap corridor. The alias is a Kings Highway segment; the target is the broader B82 Southern Brooklyn corridor across Kings Highway and Flatlands Avenue.
Excluded as partial-overlap/broader-composite corridor. The alias is a Cropsey Avenue B82 segment, while the target combines Bay Parkway and Cropsey Avenue study areas.

Excluded as same-street but different/insufficiently matched limits. The target is Cross Bay Boulevard north of 165 Av; the alias includes broader Cross Bay Boulevard bus-lane/project evidence including 107 Av to N Conduit Av.
Excluded as source-specific corridor locations/segments rather than clearly identical corridor identities.
Both plausible records are current merge targets, so neither can be used as alias under the requested guards.
Both records are current merge targets, so neither side is eligible as alias under the requested guards.
Excluded because the live rows mix broad Utica Avenue/B46 corridor identity with narrower St. Johns/Carroll/Church segments, and the safer broad records are already current merge targets.
Excluded as partial Woodhaven Boulevard segments with different limits from the broader Woodhaven Boulevard target.
Excluded because both plausible directions would require using a current merge target as alias.
Excluded because both records are current merge targets and the collision is also a local/limited/SBS route-variant lifecycle area.
Excluded because the safe lifecycle direction would use current merge target route_m23-local-cb4-apr2016 as alias; reverse direction would collapse the SBS record into the local target.
Excluded because the alias is a one-source LaGuardia workshop participant-identified Q53 route idea, not source-backed as the same Woodhaven/Cross Bay Q53 SBS service identity.

notable_exclusions.candidate: remaining route collisions for B44, B46, B82, Bx41, Bx55, M14, M34/M34A
notable_exclusions.reason: Excluded as already-prepared aliases, existing merge-target direction traps, do-not-merge-adjacent service variants, or route variants without enough source evidence to merge under the conservative route guard.

commands_run: rg read-only inspection of repo files and selected records
jq read-only inspection of data/identity-overrides/merges.json and data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json
bun --eval read-only in-process validateRepo duplicate_global_identity extraction
bun --eval read-only canonical JSONL inspection for selected route/corridor records

blockers: No high-confidence new route/corridor alias proposal survived the requested guards in batch185.

Many remaining live duplicate_global_identity rows are route service variants or lifecycle-adjacent records where existing do-not-merge semantics caution against collapsing local/limited/SBS variants without very specific source authority.

Many remaining corridor rows are same-street collisions with different limits or partial segment overlap, especially B82/Kings Highway/Flatlands/Cropsey, Cross Bay Boulevard, Utica Avenue, and Woodhaven Boulevard.
Several otherwise plausible duplicate rows require an alias_id that is already a current merge target, which the task explicitly excludes.

One later compact candidate-report validator attempt hit a transient SQLite database lock, but an earlier allowed in-process validateRepo run in this task already provided the current route/corridor duplicate_global_identity queue used for this prep.
